zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice Beauchamp於大約1240年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾時，她的父親William de Beauchamp56歲，母親Ida Longespée33歲。她於1264年之前與Thomas Fitz Otes在英國英格蘭素福爾可門德萊善姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1285年9月30日之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrice Beauchamp于大约1240年出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾时，她的父亲William de Beauchamp56岁，母亲Ida Longespée33岁。她于1264年之前与Thomas Fitz Otes在英国英格兰素福爾可門德萊善姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1285年9月30日之前。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice Beauchamp was born about 1240, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Beauchamp, was 56 and her mother, Ida Longespée, was 33. She married Thomas Fitz Otes before 1264, in Mendlesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died before 30 September 1285.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Beatrice Beauchamp est née environ 1240, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Ida Longespée, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Fitz Otes avant 1264, à Mendlesham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée avant 30 septembre 1285.

de.lifeSummary: Als Beatrice Beauchamp um 1240 in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Beauchamp 56 und ihre Mutter Ida Longespée 33. Sie hat vor 1264 in Mendlesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Thomas Fitz Otes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb vor 30. September 1285.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice Beauchamp è nata il circa 1240 nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Ida Longespée, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Fitz Otes prima del 1264, nel Mendlesham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 30 settembre 1285.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrice Beauchampが約1240年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは56歳，母Ida Longespéeは33歳であった。1264年以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクメンドレシャムでThomas Fitz Otesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1285年9月30日以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ida Longespée(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1264년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 멘들레샤에서 Thomas Fitz Otes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1285년 9월 30일 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1240, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Ida Longespée, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Fitz Otes antes 1264, em Mendlesham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 30 de setembro de 1285.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrice Beauchamp родилась около 1240 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Ida Longespée, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Fitz Otes перед 1264 г. в местности мендлешам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла перед 30 сентябрь 1285 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Beatrice Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1240, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Ida Longespée, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Thomas Fitz Otes antes de 1264, en Mendlesham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió antes de 30 de septiembre de 1285.
